description: A designed map infographic on a white/light-blue gradient background showing a gray silhouette map of the contiguous United States densely covered with colored dots clustered heavily in the Southeast (especially Florida and Texas). Top left has the DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'. A left-hand text block titled 'ICE OPERATES THREE 287(g) MODELS:' explains: 'The Jail Enforcement Model allows your officers to identify and process removable aliens who have pending or active criminal charges.'; 'The Task Force Model allows your officers to enforce limited immigration authority while performing routine police duties or as an active participant in an ICE-led task force.'; 'The Warrant Service Officer allows your officers to serve and execute administrative warrants on aliens in your custody.' A large title near the lower left reads '287(g) Participating Agencies' (with '287(g)' in a blue box). A legend at the bottom distinguishes 'Participating' agencies (solid red = Jail Enforcement Model, solid blue = Task Force Model, solid green = Warrant Service Officer) from 'Pending' agencies (lighter pastel versions of each). The messaging technique uses data-visualization to project momentum and broad nationwide adoption of ICE's 287(g) local-partnership program. Target audience: police departments and sheriffs (recruiting them to join 287(g)) and the public to demonstrate growing cooperation.
notable_text: 287(g) Participating Agencies; ICE OPERATES THREE 287(g) MODELS: Jail Enforcement Model / Task Force Model / Warrant Service Officer; Participating vs Pending
